terms.definition: Machine learning on unlabeled data to discover intrinsic structure such as clusters, latent factors, or density patterns without predicting predefined target labels.
An unsupervised task that partitions data so items within a group are more similar to each other than to items in other groups.
An unsupervised task that maps high-dimensional data to fewer dimensions while preserving meaningful structure such as variance or neighborhoods.
Hidden patterns in data—groups, manifolds, or factors—not directly observed in raw features but revealed by an unsupervised model.
Identifying data points that deviate substantially from the pattern learned from the bulk of unlabeled training data.
